beats.activity: Wake up, make espresso, review the day while rain drums on the window.
beats.reasoning: Tomas wakes early as usual. The grey drizzle outside sets the mood for a slower, more deliberate Tuesday. Yesterday's energy was 45/100—he's still recovering, so a quiet morning is right.

beats.activity: Commute to Ballard office, taking local streets to avoid SR-520 closure and morning rain.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 is closed eastbound 9am–noon, but Tomas works in Ballard (northwest), so he takes a surface-street route. Rain is steady, so he leaves early to avoid any slip-ups.

beats.activity: Sketching park designs and client calls at the office; rain keeps him indoors and focused.
beats.reasoning: Morning is prime work time. The drizzle outside makes staying at the desk feel natural rather than claustrophobic. He powers through sight-line revisions and correspondence.

beats.activity: Lunch at a cozy spot in Ballard; grabs a sandwich and coffee to fuel the afternoon.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady until 2pm, so he stays local. A quick indoor meal keeps him warm and caffeinated without wasting commute time.

beats.activity: Back to the office for presentations and detail work; rain begins to ease around 2pm.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon block dedicated to client decks and CAD refinement. By 2pm the rain tapers, but he's already in flow and stays put.

beats.activity: Commute home through clearing skies; stops by the Ballard Goodwill to browse.
beats.reasoning: Rain has cleared and Tomas is a thrift hunter. Quick 15-minute stop to hunt for vintage finds before heading home—keeps the competitive, tactile energy alive.

beats.activity: Drive home from Ballard to Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Direct route home. SR-520 closure is over by now, but he takes familiar roads anyway. Roughly 40 minutes with light evening traffic.

beats.activity: Prep and bake sourdough; tends starter, shapes loaf, lets it proof in the oven.
beats.reasoning: Sourdough is Tomas's anchor. After a long day indoors, the tactile ritual of dough-work and the oven's warmth ground him. Blunt, direct energy finds outlet in kneading.

beats.activity: Eat a late dinner, review sourdough progress, scroll through park design inspiration on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Dinner is probably bread he baked and something simple. Wind-down at home before bed; low energy from yesterday carries into a quieter evening.

diary: Rain all morning meant I actually stayed focused at the office—no excuses to wander. The SR-520 closure had everyone stressed, but taking the local streets through Ballard felt oddly peaceful. Sketched a new meadow concept for Green Lake that I'm genuinely excited about. Grabbed a sandwich at that spot on 22nd around 1, and the espresso there reminded me why I don't just make coffee at home (though I tried this morning). The afternoon presentations went smooth; by 2pm the rain softened and I could almost see the Olympics again. Stopped by Goodwill on the way home—found a vintage watering can that'll look perfect on my porch. Back home now, I've been working with my sourdough starter. There's something grounding about it, the waiting, the slow rise. Late dinner and some design scrolling on the couch. Feeling productive but tired in that good way.
thought_process: The rain actually worked in my favor today—I could've been frustrated about the commute chaos, but instead I leaned into being stuck indoors and productive. The SR-520 closure meant I had to think differently about my route, which oddly cleared my head. I was genuinely engaged with the Green Lake meadow design in a way I haven't been in weeks. The Goodwill stop wasn't planned, but I needed a little break from the work headspace before driving back to Capitol Hill. Baking sourdough felt like the right counterbalance to a day of sketches and Zoom calls—something tactile and slow. My mood shifted from the grey morning focus into something more contemplative by evening. The rain kept me grounded, literally and mentally.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
